business.headline: Got your Form 16? Check these 6 details before filing your ITRSince Form 16 forms the foundation of income reporting for salaried taxpayers, spending a few minutes verifying the information can save you a lot of trouble later.

The third dimension – after inspectors and employers, now the consultantsIn the eleventh part of this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, this article examines the role of labour consultants who, instead of ensuring compliance, sometimes help employers create the appearance of compliance while facilitating violations of labour laws.

NSE IPO filing could unlock Rs 25,000 crore for SBI, Temasek and top investorsNSE has filed draft papers for its long-delayed initial public offering after nearly a decade. The move could unlock major gains for existing investors and value the exchange at about $57 billion.

8th Pay Commission: How fitment factors from 2.57 to 3.83 may change salariesEmployee unions have pressed for a higher fitment factor as the 8th Pay Commission weighs salary and pension revision. The eventual multiplier will shape basic pay, pensions and the size of gains for millions.

Sensex closes 254 points higher, Nifty near 24,200; IndiGo up 3%The S&P BSE Sensex rose 254.36 points, or 0.33%, to close at 77,409.98, while the NSE Nifty50 gained around 82 points, or 0.34%, to settle at 24,168.

Crude oil is no longer the villain. A new threat could raise your household billEconomists say a weak monsoon has overtaken crude oil as India's main inflation risk. Poor rainfall could raise food prices, squeeze household budgets and complicate RBI rate cuts.

EPF accounts to get 8.25% interest soon after government approvalThe government has approved an 8.25% EPF interest rate for 2025-26, paving the way for account credits this month. The decision lets EPFO begin processing payments for over 7 crore subscribers after Finance Ministry clearance.

Why the Rs 30,000-crore NSE IPO is unlike any other IPOFor nearly 10 years, the NSE IPO was the market's longest-running "coming soon" story. Now, India's biggest exchange is finally ready to test its value in public.

EPFO 3.0 may let you withdraw PF through UPI, ATM soon. Here's what could changeEPFO is preparing to launch its 3.0 platform with new digital withdrawal options for subscribers. The upgrade could speed up claims and simplify access to provident fund savings.

Why Trump said 'whatever' after new US Fed chief did not cut ratesUS President Donald Trump got a new Federal Reserve chief, but not the lower-rate signal he had been hoping for. What changed was his reaction.

Infosys, TCS, HCLTech down: Why are IT stocks falling after the recent rally?IT stocks fell at the opening bell. Among the biggest losers were Infosys, which fell 2.31%, Tech Mahindra was down 1.42%, HCLTech slid 1.17%, TCS lost 1.20%, while Persistent Systems and Wipro also lost 1.68% and 0.68% respectively in early trade.

Markets open flat after 4-day rally; IT stocks fall on US rate hike concernsThe S&P BSE Sensex was down 47.00 points, or 0.06%, at 77,108.62 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 managed to stay above the key 24,000 mark, rising 2.05 points to 24,087.75 as of 9:35 am.

Did China quietly save the world from an oil shock?As US-Iran tensions stirred oil fears, China cut imports and leaned on reserves and alternative energy. The move may have eased pressure on global supplies and limited a wider price surge.

NSE's long wait ends: Exchange files IPO papers, joins Jio in mega listing raceThe National Stock Exchange (NSE) has filed draft IPO papers after a delay stretching back to 2016. The move brings one of India's biggest listings closer as the primary market regains momentum.

India to allow 3.78 lakh UK car imports at lower duties under bilateral trade pactIndia will allow imports of 3.78 lakh conventional-engine passenger cars from the UK at concessional customs duty. The provision will apply for the first 15 years after the trade pact comes into force.

Reliance Jio denies Telegram founder's cyberattack allegationsReliance Jio has dismissed Telegram founder Pavel Durov's claim that it disrupted the messaging app's access outside India through Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) hijacking. The row comes as Telegram faces a temporary block in India over alleged links to the NEET paper leak case.

India-EU trade deal to be signed by end-2026, says Von der LeyenIn a bilateral meeting between Indian and EU delegations on the sidelines of the ongoing G7 Summit in France, the two sides also resolved to step up security and defence cooperation.

UK-India trade deal to take effect on July 15 despite dispute over steel tariffsUnder the deal, India will cut whisky tariffs to 40 per cent from 150 per cent, and automotives from 10% under a quota to 100 per cent, with Britain cutting tariffs on goods such as clothes, footwear and some food.

Dubai vs India: Which real estate market gives more bang for your buck?As property prices rise across Indian metros, investors are increasingly comparing Dubai with Mumbai, Delhi-NCR and Bengaluru. Larger homes, higher yields and tax-free returns are sharpening Dubai's appeal.

IT, PSU bank stocks lead gains as markets extend rally on falling oil pricesThe S&P BSE Sensex rose 347.14 points, or 0.45%, to settle at 77,155.62, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 96.55 points, or 0.40%, to close at 24,085.70.

entertainment.headline: Akshay Kumar seeks blessings at Vaishno Devi ahead of Welcome to the Jungle Actor Akshay Kumar offered prayers at the Shri Mata Vaishno Devi Shrine under heavy security. He will be seen next in Welcome to the Jungle, releasing in theatres end of June.

Sara Ali Khan meets Henry Cavill, brings Enola Holmes vibe in vintage look. Pics Sara Ali Khan shared photographs with Henry Cavill from a Longines campaign styled in a vintage mood. The pairing has drawn attention as a new East-meets-West crossover with an Enola Holmes-like feel.

Director Raghavendra Rao pulls actors Keerthy Suresh, Krithi Shetty closer; slammed A video from director Anil Ravipudi's Hyderabad film launch shows veteran filmmaker K Raghavendra Rao pulling actors Keerthy Suresh and Krithi Shetty closer by holding their arms. The clip triggered criticism online, with users slamming him for his inappropriate behaviour.

Cocktail 2 box office prediction: Pre-sales lower than Tere Ishk Mein, Thamma Shahid Kapoor's Cocktail 2 has earned over Rs 3 crore ahead of its June 19 release. Box office trade estimates suggest a moderate start, with advance bookings trailing Kriti Sanon and Rashmika Mandanna's Tere Ishk Mein and Thamma respectively.

Ram Charan's Peddi gets 6 minutes of new footage: What's been added and edited Nearly two weeks after its release, Peddi's theatrical version is updated with close to six minutes of additional footage. Fans say the new version restores some important scenes while removing a few controversial moments.

Dhanush's D55 with Rajkumar Periasamy titled Om, teaser hints at social unrest Actor Dhanush and director Rajkumar Periasamy's film has been titled Om. The makers announced the title along with a gritty first-look teaser on Thursday.

I, Nobody trailer: Prithviraj Sukumaran plays a man caught in a web of crime The official trailer of I, Nobody introduces Prithviraj Sukumaran as Rajeevan, a man trapped in crime and personal turmoil. The promo points to a character-led thriller where family ties and psychological conflict drive the suspense.

Tanya Mittal defends Pranit More amid social media backlash: Feel bad for him Bigg Boss 19 contestant Tanya Mittal said she feels bad for comedian Pranit More even as she called his conduct wrong. Her remarks come as the row over the viral crowd-work clip has led to FIRs, apologies and a consent debate.

Director Raj Nidimoru on bodyguard duty for wife Samantha during Tirupati visit Raj Nidimoru showed his protective side during a visit to Tirumala temple with wife Samantha Ruth Prabhu, gently shielding her from the crowd after offering prayers. The couple is gearing up for the release of their upcoming film Maa Inti Bangaaram.

Bombay High Court permits Preity Zinta to sue Google, Meta over AI deepfakes The Bombay High Court has allowed Preity Zinta to move ahead with a civil suit against Google, Meta, X Corp and other digital entities over alleged deepfakes and altered content. The case centres on claims of personality rights, copyright and moral rights violations.

Maanvi Gagroo recalls being offered Rs 1 lakh to 'compromise' early in her career Actor Maanvi Gagroo recalled receiving a text offering more than Rs 1 lakh to 'compromise' at the start of her career. She said the episode showed how difficult Bollywood can be for outsiders.

Doraemon is heading to theatres in India, and the movie date is finally locked Doraemon is finally making its big screen debut in India with the release of Doraemon the Movie: New Nobita and the Castle of the Undersea Devil. Fans can look forward to an exciting underwater adventure in Hindi, Tamil, and Telugu.

Imtiaz Ali's dream project? A Radha-Krishna love story with 'deeper philosophy' Imtiaz Ali said he wants to bring the story of Radha and Krishna to the big screen. In a recent interview, the filmmaker called it a tale with many layers and a deeper philosophy beyond romance.

Actor Aasif Sheikh draws line after Pranit More row: Humiliation is not comedy Actor Aasif Sheikh draws a line after Pranit More's row, saying humiliation is not comedy and offensive jokes should be avoided. The actor also urged comedians to focus on their craft rather than relying on controversy for short-term popularity.

Alpha action director defends Alia amid trolling, says she went above and beyond Alpha action director Craig Macrae praised Alia Bhatt and Sharvari for their rigorous preparation for the film. He said their discipline gave the stunt team freedom to design more demanding action sequences.

Saif Ali Khan honours Tiger Pataudi at Winchester College plaque ceremony Saif Ali Khan attended the unveiling of a commemorative plaque for Tiger Pataudi at Winchester College. The ceremony honoured Pataudi's cricketing legacy and underlined the institution's long-standing links with India.

Legal notice to the makers of Kajal Aggarwal's The India Story over agriculture claims The makers of Kajal Aggarwal's The India Story have received a legal notice over claims in its teaser. The complainant has sought changes, asked CBFC to withhold certification and warned of further action.

Zeenat Aman reveals her Hindu name, discusses faith and why she isn't religious Zeenat Aman shared that she also has a Hindu name and explained why she does not consider herself a religious person. Her candid remarks on faith and religion came at the Amrit Ratna 2026 summit in New Delhi on Thursday.

Kala Hiran actor who quit film says he was cast for Salman link, Rajasthan roots Actor Sonu Mmishra has challenged Kala Hiran director Bharat S Shrinate's claim that he was never part of the film. He has proved their association and also claimed he was cast because of his link with Salman Khan.

general news.headline: Mojtaba Khamenei says 'desperate' Trump used all kinds of leverage for Iran dealIn a first public response since the US-Iran draft agreement was signed virtually, Khamenei said he authorised the agreement after officials pledged to safeguard "the rights of the Iranian nation and the resistance front".

How Sena UBT rebellion cements Shinde's dominance in Maharashtra politicsThe rebellion in the Uddhav Sena significantly bolsters Eknath Shinde's clout within the NDA, strengthening his position in Maharashtra politics and enhancing his bargaining power in New Delhi.

JD Vance slams Israeli 'freakout' over Iran deal, says Trump is your only allyJD Vance rebuked Israeli critics of the US-Iran deal and defended Donald Trump's approach. His unusually sharp remarks landed as Israeli officials, including some of Benjamin Netanyahu's cabinet ministers, continued to attack the deal.

CAN 3-0 QAT Live: David brace puts Canada in cruise control against 10-man QatarCanada vs Qatar Live, FIFA World Cup 2026: Cyle Larin and Jonathan David have put hosts Canada in cruise control against Qatar in Vancouver. Canada are eyeing their first World Cup win and are in command.
Anti-defection law has failed, opacity nahin chalegiA demand for transparency in the world's largest democracy.

US lifts naval blockade on all maritime traffic entering and leaving Iranian portsThe move is expected to ease concerns over shipping disruptions in and around Iranian waters and could provide relief to commercial operators navigating key maritime routes in the region.

South Africa's Cristiano Ronaldo tribute goes viral after Mokoena's late penaltyFIFA World Cup 2026: South Africa's Teboho Mokoena marked his late equaliser against Czechia with Cristiano Ronaldo's trademark celebrations. The moment highlighted Ronaldo's enduring pull even as Portugal opened their World Cup with a frustrating draw.

Architect of turnaround: How Sergio Gor rescued US-India relationshipA handshake that took 16 months to materialise became the defining moment in Sergio Gor's effort to reset US-India ties. The episode cast him as the central figure in a diplomatic turnaround.

Priy mitra Narendra: Macron surprises with Hindi farewell to PM ModiFrench President Emmanuel Macron surprised Prime Minister Narendra Modi with a farewell message in Hindi, calling him a "dear friend" and celebrating the enduring India-France partnership, while praising the success of his France visit.

What a rotten deal we made with Iran: How Trump's 2013 tweet turns propheticTrump once called a 2013 Iran nuclear deal rotten and vowed to negotiate something better. After dismantling the Obama-era accord and confronting Tehran militarily, he has secured a deal that opponents say exceeds the concessions he once condemned.

Lionel Messi's father recovering from health issue, family slams false reportsLionel Messi's family said his father Jorge, is recovering under medical supervision. The statement also condemned rumours and urged privacy after intense speculation.

Can Netanyahu derail Trump's Iran deal? Inside Israel's push to influence USBenjamin Netanyahu is trying to shape the final terms of the US-Iran memorandum after its signing. The push centres on nuclear restrictions, Lebanon and widening differences with Donald Trump.

Refinery up in flames as 200 drones swarm Moscow in biggest strike by UkraineUkrainian drones unleashed chaos in Moscow, with at least seven striking a key oil refinery just 15 km from the Kremlin, triggering massive fires and plumes of black smoke. The attack involved nearly 200 drones and was the largest on Moscow in two years.

SP split real or BJP's mind game? Decoding UP defection buzz ahead of 2027 pollsWhile the BJP claims that up to 30 Samajwadi Party MPs could defect, the Akhilesh Yadav-led SP has dismissed it as a mere political mind game.

NDA-backed candidate wins Jharkhand Rajya Sabha polls after cross-votingNDA-backed Independent Parimal Nathwani won a Rajya Sabha seat from Jharkhand amid cross-voting, while JMM's Baidyanath Ram took the second berth. Nathwani's victory came despite the NDA being short of the required numbers in the Assembly.

Self-styled godman tortures, rapes woman for years, forces her to drink his urinePune Police have arrested self-styled spiritual guru Radhamohan Mishra and seven associates after a woman alleged years of sexual exploitation and harassment. Investigators say seized devices, CCTV material and an underground tunnel under construction are now central to the probe.

Dhanush's D55 with Rajkumar Periasamy titled Om, teaser hints at social unrestActor Dhanush and director Rajkumar Periasamy's film has been titled Om. The makers announced the title along with a gritty first-look teaser on Thursday.

Western Disturbance to drench the north even as heat grips central India todayThe IMD says a western disturbance will bring rain and thunderstorms to north India on Friday, June 19, while central India swelters under heatwave conditions. The northeast braces for heavy to very heavy rain as the monsoon advances.

Israel's interests compromised: Envoy on deal leaving out Iran ballistic missilesIsrael's envoy to India, Reuven Azar, in an exclusive interview with India Today, picked holes in the US's interim peace deal with Iran. Azar said Israel's security concerns had not been adequately addressed, especially concerning Lebanon and Iran's ballistic missiles.

India-born Balwinder became Jaswinder for US citizenship. This happened nextAn India-born man, Balwinder Singh, who changed his name to Jaswinder Singh to obtain immigration benefits and eventually US citizenship, is facing a civil lawsuit. The federal complaint seeks to revoke Singh's naturalisation, claiming he obtained US citizenship through deception and misrepresentation.

Cocktail 2 box office prediction: Pre-sales lower than Tere Ishk Mein, ThammaShahid Kapoor's Cocktail 2 has earned over Rs 3 crore ahead of its June 19 release. Box office trade estimates suggest a moderate start, with advance bookings trailing Kriti Sanon and Rashmika Mandanna's Tere Ishk Mein and Thamma respectively.

Conversion, terror funding? Why India is probing US missionary Timothy InitiativeThe Timothy Initiative (TTI), a US-based evangelical organisation, has been charged with funnelling over Rs 92 crore into India through 1,000 foreign debit cards, bypassing FCRA rules. With funds traced to Maoist-affected regions, UAPA has been invoked. There are allegations that TTI was involved in religious conversions.

UK's latest visa changes have Indian students taking a closer look at the UK dreamThe UK government's tougher student visa measures have raised fresh questions among Indian students planning to study abroad. While the crackdown targets universities rather than applicants, experts say it is accelerating a shift towards smarter decision-making, with students focusing on employability, career outcomes and value for money.

Monsoon has hit a wall: Here's what needs to change for rain to returnIndia's monsoon advance has stalled for nearly two weeks after an early onset. Forecasts suggest weakening dry air and stronger winds could restart its spread in the coming days.

sports.headline: Shreyanka ruled out of T20 World Cup, India name leg-spinner as replacement Shreyanka Patil has been ruled out of the Women's T20 World Cup after injuring her ankle against the Netherlands. India have brought in uncapped leg-spinner Prema Rawat as the replacement for the bowling all-rounder.

Super-sub Manzambi fires Switzerland to 4-1 win vs Bosnia and Herzegovina FIFA World Cup 2026: Johan Manzambi came off the bench to score twice as Switzerland beat Bosnia and Herzegovina 4-1 in Los Angeles. The win put Switzerland in charge of Group B and left Bosnia under pressure ahead of Qatar.

Ivory Coast's Elye Wahi gets Canada entry despite spot-fixing probe Ivory Coast forward Elye Wahi has been cleared to travel to Canada after his authorisation came through. The late approval arrived amid a French betting investigation, though he has not been named a suspect.

CAN 3-0 QAT Live: David brace puts Canada in cruise control against 10-man Qatar Canada vs Qatar Live, FIFA World Cup 2026: Cyle Larin and Jonathan David have put hosts Canada in cruise control against Qatar in Vancouver. Canada are eyeing their first World Cup win and are in command.

ENG vs NZ: Glenn Phillips, Matt Henry turn the screws on England at the Oval ENG vs NZ, 2nd Test Day 2: Glenn Phillips scored his maiden Test century and Matt Henry struck twice as New Zealand moved ahead on day two at The Oval. England closed on 222 for six, still 169 behind and under pressure in the second Test.

Thomas Tuchel crashout forces FIFA to change World Cup anthem protocol Thomas Tuchel's complaint about blocked views during the national anthem prompted FIFA to reposition photographers at World Cup 2026 venues. The change followed England's win over Croatia in Dallas and addressed a pre-match issue caused by the stadium layout.

South Africa's Cristiano Ronaldo tribute goes viral after Mokoena's late penalty FIFA World Cup 2026: South Africa's Teboho Mokoena marked his late equaliser against Czechia with Cristiano Ronaldo's trademark celebrations. The moment highlighted Ronaldo's enduring pull even as Portugal opened their World Cup with a frustrating draw.

Czechia let victory slip as South Africa strike late to earn a draw in Atlanta FIFA World Cup 2026: Teboho Mokoena's late penalty rescued a 1-1 draw for South Africa against Czechia after Michal Sadilek's early strike, keeping both teams alive but under pressure in Group A.

Lionel Messi's father recovering from health issue, family slams false reports Lionel Messi's family said his father Jorge, is recovering under medical supervision. The statement also condemned rumours and urged privacy after intense speculation.

SUI 4-1 BIH Highlights: Manzambi at the double as Swiss thrash Bosnia Switzerland (SUI) vs Bosnia and Herzegovina (BIH) Highlights, FIFA World Cup: Manzambi's brace gives Switzerland all three points against 10-man Bosnia and Herzegovina to get their first win of the campaign. Ruben Vargas and Granit Xhaka rounded off a sensational second half performance that blew away Bosnia.

Neymar not travelling with Brazil for Haiti clash as injury rehab continuesFIFA World Cup: Brazil will be without Neymar for their second World Cup group match against Haiti as the forward continues his recovery from a calf injury, remaining in New Jersey to complete the final stage of rehabilitation.

Dropped by England after nightclub brawl, Ben Stokes returns to cricketBen Stokes has been named in Durham's squad days after England dropped him over a nightclub altercation. The county return comes as ECB investigations continue into the curfew breach and its impact on his Test future.

Pakistan players set for pay hike despite winning just two Tests last yearThe PCB has finalised new format-based central contracts with higher payments for Track A Test players. The plan also adds event-win bonuses and faces criticism over possible secrecy around categories.

Shades for Archer, history for New Zealand: Glenn Phillips completes century trebleGlenn Phillips scored his maiden Test hundred at The Oval after standing up to Jofra Archer's hostile spell. The innings took New Zealand to 391 and made him only the third Kiwi batter with international centuries in all three formats.

Ivory Coast's Elye Wahi denied entry to Canada amid spot-fixing probeIvory Coast striker Elye Wahi cannot enter Canada for Saturday's World Cup match against Germany. The setback follows a French spot-fixing investigation and forces coach Emerse Fae to alter plans.

Table tennis star Manika Batra dropped from Asian Games squadManika Batra has been left out of India's Asian Games table tennis squad after missing the selection criteria of the sport's governing body in India. She has been named a reserve, while a rankings-based squad will be led by Sreeja Akula.

Neeraj Chopra returns to Doha with old style, sets sights on Asian Games, CWGEight months on from an injury-hit eighth at Tokyo's World Championships, Neeraj Chopra opens his season in Doha, training now under his first coach and chasing the throw that always felt most like him.

Why BCCI allowed Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's parents to travel with him for India tours?BCCI has allowed 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's parents to accompany him on India's white-ball tour of Ireland and England. The board says the move will ease his entry into a senior dressing room and that any on-field disciplinary matters belong with match officials.

Raaj Tilak: India A captain rights his wrong in Sri LankaThere is a new leader in the making in India's cricketing ecosystem. Tilak Varma, a product of Mumbai Indians, has shown immense maturity and the rare ability to right the wrongs in a space of a few hours. Could he become India's long-time captain in the future?

Hiding behind Cristiano Ronaldo? Bruno Fernandes' DR Congo no-show raises questionsPortugal's 1-1 draw with DR Congo reopened the debate over Cristiano Ronaldo after another scoreless night. But the backlash also exposed wider attacking failures, with Bruno Fernandes and Portugal's creators unable to supply the final pass.

I think Ishan Kishan has already got his South African visa: Sunil GavaskarSunil Gavaskar said Ishan Kishan's 125 against Afghanistan has nearly sealed his place in India's 2027 ODI plans. He and Graeme Swann praised Kishan's improved range, flexibility and growing authority.

Ronaldo's fear factor fading? DR Congo defender's candid remarks raise eyebrowsCristiano Ronaldo's standing as Portugal's main attacking threat came under the spotlight after DR Congo midfielder Ngal'ayel Mukau revealed his side had no special plan to stop the veteran forward in their FIFA World Cup 2026 clash.

Summers too hot for cricket? BCCI considers starting IPL 2027 in early MarchThe BCCI is considering moving the IPL to a March 10 to May 15 window to avoid late-season heat and pre-monsoon rain. The plan keeps the tournament at 74 matches for now and would require domestic cricket to finish earlier.

Sub Cristiano Ronaldo when I need goals? Portugal coach laments doubtersFIFA World Cup 2026: Portugal opened with a 1-1 draw against DR Congo as Cristiano Ronaldo made his sixth World Cup appearance. The result sharpened doubts over their attacking balance and their continuing dependence on a 41-year-old captain.

Watch: Stylish Phillips dons sunglasses to survive Archer barrage at The OvalGlenn Phillips produced one of the most memorable moments of the opening day of the second Test at The Oval when he called for a pair of sunglasses to deal with the glare from the sightscreen before facing a fierce spell from Jofra Archer.

Brar credits domestic cricket and India A stint for strong international startGurnoor Brar has announced himself in the Afghanistan ODI series with pace, bounce and six wickets in two matches. His seamless jump from Ranji and India A underlines how India's system keeps producing fast bowlers ready for international cricket.

Luis Diaz sparks Colombia to commanding 3-1 win over debutants UzbekistanLuis Diaz starred with a goal and an assist as Colombia kicked off their FIFA World Cup 2026 campaign with a commanding 3-1 win over tournament debutants Uzbekistan in Group K at a packed Azteca Stadium on Wednesday.

Messi 3-0 Ronaldo after World Cup Round 1: Did we not see it coming?FIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi arrived at the World Cup carrying a legacy and somehow made it bigger. Cristiano Ronaldo arrived chasing history, but after Round 1, the tournament feels less like a race between rivals and more like a tale of two timelines.

Mexican military shoots down drone near South Korea's training campMexican military personnel neutralised an unauthorised drone near South Korea's training base in Guadalajara ahead of the team's Group A clash against hosts Mexico at the FIFA World Cup 2026.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
